data.body:
PASS-1 review (criterion T5c — Security overlay): PASS, no findings.

The plan crosses several real trust boundaries and makes an EXPLICIT, sound auth decision for each:
- Public ingress (nginx :443): the only public surface; TLS-terminating reverse proxy, `/review/` gated on the bot token by the S4b receiver.
- Gerrit HTTP :8080 / SSH :29418: Gerrit's own authenticated access; SSM Session Manager as the sole admin path (no inbound SSH).
- Inbound webhook receiver: two-layer control (private docker-compose network as PRIMARY gate — receiver loopback-bound 127.0.0.1:8000, port not in the SG; secret-URL token as SECONDARY defence-in-depth; receiver-side token+source validation), with an honest, documented rationale for the absence of HMAC (webhooks plugin has none) — ADR-0014.
- Secrets lifecycle: SSM Parameter Store SecureString pulled to a chmod-600 .env at boot; single rotatable bot token; committed webhooks.config carries only a `__BOT_TOKEN__` placeholder (no plaintext secret in IaC); token not replicated to GitHub (replicatePermissions=false).
- Least-privilege: instance-role SSM read scoped to arn:...:parameter/rebar/prod/* (no account wildcard), kms:Decrypt constrained by kms:ViaService; replication identity is a write-only single-repo deploy key and the sole ruleset bypass actor.
- Fail-closed voter (BLOCK/error -> -1).

No undeclared or ambiguous auth posture on any reachable service. Nothing to remediate for T5c.
